name: Lakshmana
description: Lakshmana, a central figure in the Hindu epic Ramayana, is revered as the embodiment of selfless service, unwavering loyalty, and righteous indignation. He is the younger brother and inseparable companion of Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu. Born to King Dasharatha and Queen Sumitra, Lakshmana's devotion to Rama is a cornerstone of his character, leading him to renounce his own comforts and royal entitlements to accompany Rama and Sita into exile. He acts as Rama's protector and enforcer, his fierce temper often serving as a counterbalance to Rama's serene composure. Lakshmana is not merely a follower but an active participant in the epic's events, his skills as an archer and warrior proving essential in the fight against Ravana's forces. His role highlights the importance of brotherhood, duty, and the sacrifices made in the pursuit of dharma. While sometimes impulsive, Lakshmana's actions are consistently motivated by a desire to protect Rama and uphold righteousness, solidifying his place as a revered figure in Hindu tradition.
subtitle: The Loyal Brother

keyMyths.title: Lakshmana's Renunciation
keyMyths.description: Upon Rama's exile, Lakshmana vehemently insists on accompanying his brother and Sita to the forest, forsaking his own wife, Urmila, and the comforts of the palace. He argues that his duty lies with Rama and pledges to protect them from all dangers. This decision highlights his unwavering devotion and his willingness to endure hardship for the sake of his brother's well-being. Lakshmana's choice sets the stage for his crucial role in the Ramayana's unfolding narrative.

keyMyths.title: The Lakshmana Rekha
keyMyths.description: While Rama is away hunting, Sita is left alone in their forest dwelling. Lakshmana draws a protective line (the Lakshmana Rekha) around the hut and instructs Sita not to cross it for her own safety. When Ravana appears disguised as a mendicant and begs for alms, Sita, compelled by dharma, crosses the line, making herself vulnerable to abduction. This event demonstrates the limitations of even the most protective measures and the importance of heeding warnings.

keyMyths.title: Lakshmana's Coma and Hanuman's Intervention
keyMyths.description: During the battle with Ravana's son, Indrajit, Lakshmana is struck by a powerful weapon and falls into a coma. His life is saved by Hanuman, who flies to the Himalayas to retrieve the Sanjeevani herb, which possesses the power to revive the fallen warrior. This episode showcases Lakshmana's vulnerability, the severity of the battle, and Hanuman's crucial role as a devoted ally of Rama and Lakshmana.

sources.author: Valmiki
